prompt: The batch loader resolves all promises with the same value instead of mapping results back to individual requests

turns.content:
<task>
The batch loader resolves all promises with the same value instead of mapping results back to individual requests
</task>

FAIL  src/__tests__/loader.test.ts
  BatchLoader
    ✓ loads single item (5 ms)
    ✕ loads multiple items with correct mapping (8 ms)

  ● BatchLoader › loads multiple items with correct mapping

    expect(received).toEqual(expected)

    Expected: {"id": 1, "name": "Alice"}
    Received: [{"id": 1, "name": "Alice"}, {"id": 2, "name": "Bob"}]

      14 |     const [user1, user2] = await Promise.all([p1, p2]);
      15 |     expect(user1).toEqual({ id: 1, name: 'Alice' });

Tests:       1 failed, 1 passed, 2 total

arguments.reasoning: Found the bug at line 29: `item.resolve(results as any)` resolves EVERY promise with the entire results array. It should resolve each promise with its corresponding result: `batch[i].resolve(results[i])`. The dispatch loop iterates over batch items but doesn't use the index to map to the correct result.

arguments.summary: Found bug in dispatch() line 28-29: resolves all promises with the full results array instead of mapping each batch item to its corresponding result by index.

next_steps.description: Fix dispatch: change `for (const item of batch)` to indexed loop `batch.forEach((item, i) => item.resolve(results[i]))` to correctly map results
Run jest to verify both tests pass

turns.content:
PASS  src/__tests__/loader.test.ts
  BatchLoader
    ✓ loads single item (4 ms)
    ✓ loads multiple items with correct mapping (3 ms)

Test Suites: 1 passed, 1 total
Tests:       2 passed, 2 total

arguments.summary: Fixed batch result mapping in dispatch(): changed from resolving all promises with entire results array to mapping each batch item to its corresponding result by index using forEach. Both tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed BatchLoader in src/loader.ts: dispatch() was resolving all promises with the entire results array (`item.resolve(results)`) instead of the corresponding result by index. Changed to `batch.forEach((item, i) => item.resolve(results[i]))`. Both tests pass.
